title: Qualcomm's Automotive Design-Win Pipeline Expands to $30 Billion
text: – Greater than 2X Increase in Pipeline Since November 2021  – – Snapdragon Digital Chassis Enables the Car of the Future –  NEW YORK , Sept. 22, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Qualcomm Incorporated (NASDAQ: QCOM) announced at its inaugural Automotive Investor Day that the Company's automotive design-win pipeline has grown to $30 billion 1, driven by increased adoption of its Snapdragon® Digital Chassis™ solutions across the auto industry.

title: CHIPS Act Update Gives Bears the Upper Hand With Chip Stocks
text: Semiconductor stocks rose over the weekend after details emerged on how the CHIPS and Science Act will be allocated. title: Is Qualcomm Setting Itself Up for Failure?

text: Many industries served by semiconductor companies have not been able to get the chips they needed and have suffered huge financial losses. Qualcomm is in the midst of a digital supply chain transformation to improve their ability to meet their customer's needs.

title: Meta, Qualcomm strike agreement on custom virtual reality chips
text: Meta Platforms Inc signed an agreement to have chip-maker Qualcomm Inc produce custom chipsets for its Quest virtual reality devices, the companies announced at a consumer electronics conference in Berlin on Friday.

title: Why Arm's lawsuit against Qualcomm is a big deal
text: Arm-based chips have been ascendant as they are more power-efficient than the x86-based chips made by Intel and AMD.

title: Qualcomm sued by longtime partner Arm over high-performance chip technology
text: Arm Limited, a heavyweight technology partner of Qualcomm for years, has accused the San Diego company of violating chip architecture intellectual property licenses linked to a new line of high-performance processors for laptops, digital cars, smartphones and even data center servers.

title: Qualcomm (QCOM) Faces Chipmaker Arm Lawsuit for Contract Breach
text: The bone of contention relates to Qualcomm's (QCOM) Nuvia buyout for $1.4 billion in 2021 and designs developed under Nuvia???s license agreements with Arm.

title: SoftBank's Arm Sues Qualcomm Over Nuvia Chip Designs
text: Arm, the U.K.-based chipmaker owned by SoftBank, filed a lawsuit against U.S. rival Qualcomm demanding that it destroy Nuvia semiconductors based on Arm's architecture.

title: Arm sues Qualcomm for license breach
text: British chip designer ARM Holdings, owned by Softbank Group, said it has sued QUALCOMM, Inc. (NASDAQ:QCOM) and its recently acquired chip design firm Nuvia Inc for breaching license agreements and trademark infringement.  Arm is seeking an injunction to compel Qualcomm to destroy designs developed under Nuvia's license agreements with Arm, as Qualcomm allegedly was required to receive Arm's approval before these could be transferred to Qualcomm.
